name: Logical Clocks (Lamport & Vector)
def: Counters that order events by causality rather than wall-clock time across distributed nodes.
problem: Establish a consistent "happens-before" ordering of events when clocks are unsynchronised and there is no global time.
mechanism: A Lamport clock increments per event and on receive takes max(local, sent)+1, giving a total order consistent with causality. A vector clock keeps one counter per node, capturing causality exactly and detecting concurrent events.
benefits: Foundation for CRDTs, snapshots and replication
limits: Vector clocks grow O(N) with node count
failures: Inferring causality from Lamport timestamps (they cannot)
Unbounded vector-clock growth with churning membership
whenNot: When physical timestamps (or hybrid logical clocks) are required
myths: "Timestamps order distributed events" — wall clocks skew; only logical clocks track causality
